title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Alchemist" book by Paulo Coelho?
Options:
A. The boy observed the sun starting its descent, disappearing behind the white buildings bordering the plaza. He recalled that when the sun had risen that day, he was on another landmass, still a shepherd with sixty sheep, looking ahead to encountering a girl. That morning he had foreseen everything that would happen to him as he walked through the familiar meadows. But now, as the sun began its sunset, he was in a foreign country, an outsider in an unfamiliar place, where he didn't even comprehend the language. He was no longer a shepherd, and had nothing, not even the money to go back and restart.
B. The sun began its departure, as well. The boy watched it through its trajectory for some time, until it was hidden behind the white houses surrounding the plaza. He recalled that when the sun had risen that morning, he was on another continent, still a shepherd with sixty sheep, and looking forward to meeting with a girl. That morning he had known everything that was going to happen to him as he walked through the familiar fields. But now, as the sun began to set, he was in a different country, a stranger in a strange land, where he couldn’t even speak the language. He was no longer a shepherd, and he had nothing, not even the money to return and start everything over. 
C. As the sun started to set, the boy observed it disappearing behind the white buildings around the plaza. That morning when the sun had risen, he was in another country, still a shepherd with his sixty sheep, and was looking forward to seeing a girl. Back then he had known everything that would happen as he walked through the familiar fields. But now, with the sunset, he was in a foreign land where he didn't know the language. He was no longer a shepherd, and had nothing, not even money to go back home and start over.
D. The boy watched as the sun began to set, vanishing behind the white houses encircling the plaza. He remembered that morning when the sun had emerged, he was on a different continent, still a shepherd with sixty sheep, and excited to meet with a girl. That morning he had anticipated everything that would transpire as he strolled through the well-known fields. However now, as the sun started to disappear, he was in an unfamiliar country, a stranger in a strange place, where he didn't even know the language. He was no longer a shepherd, and had nothing, not even the funds to return and begin anew. 
Answer:
